lang.desc: Free and open-sourced terminal/ssh/sftp/telnet/serialport/RDP/VNC/Spice/ftp client (linux, mac, win)
lang.featuresSubtitle: Everything you need in a terminal client
lang.featureAllInOneDesc: Free and open-sourced terminal, file manager, and SSH/SFTP/FTP/Telnet/Serial port/RDP/VNC/Spice client in one app.
lang.featureAiAssistantDesc: Works with any OpenAI-compatible LLM API. Generate commands, explain commands, generate scripts, create bookmarks from natural language, and run tasks directly in Agent mode.
lang.featureSecureAuthDesc: Supports publicKey + password authentication, global and per-session proxy, SSH tunneling, Agent forwarding, port forwarding, X11 forwarding, and connection hopping.
lang.featureRemoteFileEditingDesc: Double-click to directly edit remote files via integrated SFTP file manager with drag and drop.
lang.featureMultiTabTerminalDesc: Multiple terminal sessions in tabs with split panes, quick input to one or all terminals.
lang.featureGlobalHotkeyDesc: Toggle window visibility with a global hotkey (default Ctrl+2), similar to Guake-style drop terminals.
lang.featureThemesCustomizationDesc: UI/terminal themes, terminal background images, transparent windows on Mac and Windows.
lang.featureMultiLanguageDesc: 14+ languages via community translations. Contributions and fixes welcome.
lang.featureMcpDeepLinksDesc: MCP widget for AI assistants and external tools. Open connections via URLs like ssh://user@host:22.
lang.featureCloudSyncDesc: Sync bookmarks, themes, and quick commands to GitHub/Gitee secret gists, WebDAV, or custom servers.
lang.featureZmodemTrzszDesc: Supports Zmodem (rz/sz) and Trzsz (trz/tsz) file transfer, compatible with tmux.
lang.downloadSubtitle: Free and open-source — choose your platform (Windows, macOS, Linux)
lang.changelogSubtitle: What's new in the latest version
lang.communityWechatDesc: Join our WeChat community for support and updates
lang.footerBrandDesc: Free and open-source terminal/SSH/SFTP/Telnet/Serial port/RDP/VNC/Spice client for Linux, macOS, and Windows.
lang.sponsorSubtitle: Help support continued development of this open source project
lang.sponsorWechatDonateDesc: Scan the QR code with WeChat to donate directly
lang.videosSubtitle: Learn how to use electerm with video tutorials
lang.demoSubtitle: Try electerm directly in your browser — no installation needed
lang.resourcesSubtitle: Everything you need to get started
lang.sponsorsInterested: Interested in sponsoring electerm? Your support helps keep the project active and growing.
lang.sponsorsContact: Contact us to become a sponsor
lang.faqSubtitle: Common questions and answers about electem terminal client
lang.faqDisclaimer: These answers are based on the
lang.faqQ0A1: Yes! electerm is completely free and open-source software. You can view, modify, and contribute to the source code on GitHub:
lang.faqQ0A2: It is released under the MIT License, which means you are free to use, copy, modify, merge, publish, distribute, sublicense, and/or sell copies of the software.
lang.faqQ1: Can I hide the local file manager in the SFTP panel?
lang.faqQ1A1: Yes, add the following CSS to Custom CSS in Settings (Settings → Appearance → Custom CSS):
lang.faqQ1A2: After saving, the local section will be hidden and the remote section will expand to full width.
lang.faqQ2: Can I swap the local and remote sections in the SFTP panel?
lang.faqQ2A: Yes, use the following Custom CSS to place the remote section on the left and the local section on the right:
lang.faqQ3: How do I keep my SSH session from timing out?
lang.faqQ3A1: There are two methods to prevent idle SSH connections from being dropped:
lang.faqQ3A2: Method 1 — Configure Keep Alive Interval in Settings:
lang.faqQ3A3: Method 2 — Click the keep-alive icon in the terminal toolbar during an active session. This sends an Enter key every 3 seconds (more aggressive, may interfere with interactive commands like vim).
lang.faqQ4: How do I use SSH tunnels in electerm?
lang.faqQ4A1: SSH tunnels are configured when creating or editing an SSH bookmark. You can set up:
lang.faqQ4A2Desc: Forward requests from a local port to a remote server port.
lang.faqQ4A3Desc: Forward requests from a remote port to a local server port.
lang.faqQ5: What is the Workspace feature and how do I use it?
lang.faqQ5A1: Workspace lets you save your current layout and open tabs as a preset. Only tabs opened from bookmarks are saved.
lang.faqQ5A2Li1: Set your desired layout via the Layout dropdown in the tab bar
lang.faqQ5A2Li2: Open the bookmarked connections you want included
lang.faqQ5A2Li3: Go to Layout → Workspace tab → Save Workspace
lang.faqQ6: How do I connect to a server without saving a bookmark?
lang.faqQ6A1: Use Quick Connect from the sidebar. Enter a connection string like:
lang.faqQ6A2: Quick Connect supports SSH, Telnet, VNC, RDP, Spice, Serial, and FTP protocols. You can skip the protocol prefix for SSH connections.
lang.faqQ7: What is Batch Operation and how do I use it?
lang.faqQ7A1: Batch Operation lets you define and run multi-step SSH/SFTP workflows using JSON. Access it via the Batch Operation button on the left sidebar.
lang.faqQ7A2Connect: — Connects to a remote SSH server
lang.faqQ8: Can I open SSH connections from a browser or external links?
lang.faqQ8A1: Yes, electerm supports deep links using protocols like ssh://, telnet://, rdp://, vnc://, serial://, spice://, and the custom electerm:// protocol. Register protocol handlers in electerm settings first.
lang.faqQ8A2: You can also create desktop shortcuts, HTML links, or use command-line tools (open, xdg-open, start) to launch connections.
lang.faqQ9: How do I make electerm start automatically when I log in?
lang.faqQ9A1: Use the --autohide flag to start electerm minimized to the system tray:
lang.faqQ9A2UbuntuDesc: Open Startup Applications, add electerm with the command: electerm --autohide
lang.faqQ9A3WinDesc: Create a shortcut to electerm.exe and place it in %appdata%\Microsoft\Windows\Start Menu\Programs\Startup
lang.faqQ9A4MacDesc: Create a script with /Applications/electerm.app/Contents/MacOS/electerm --autohide, make it executable, then add it in System Settings → Login Items
lang.faqQ10: How do I configure AI assistant in electerm?
lang.faqQ10A1: Electerm works with any OpenAI-compatible API. To configure:
lang.faqQ10A2Li2: Enter the API URL for your provider (e.g. https://api.openai.com/v1)
lang.faqQ10A2Li3: Select or type the model name (e.g. gpt-4, deepseek-chat)
lang.faqQ10A2Li5: Optionally define a system role prompt and save
lang.faqQ11: Can I use dynamic values in quick commands?
lang.faqQ11A1: Yes, quick command templates support the following dynamic placeholders that resolve at execution time:
lang.faqQ11A2Clipboard: Clipboard — inserts the current clipboard content
lang.faqQ12: Can I set a default profile for new bookmarks?
lang.faqQ12A: Yes. Enable the 'Default' checkbox on a profile in electerm settings. That profile will be automatically selected when creating new bookmarks.
lang.homeFaqSubtitle: Quick answers to common questions about electerm
lang.homeFaq0A: Yes! electerm is completely free and open-source (MIT license). View the source on GitHub.
lang.homeFaq1Q: How do I keep my SSH session from timing out?
lang.homeFaq1A: Set SSH Keep Alive Interval in Settings, or click the keep-alive icon in the terminal toolbar during an active session.
lang.homeFaq2Q: Can I use SSH tunnels in electerm?
lang.homeFaq2A: Yes, configure local → remote or remote → local port forwarding when creating or editing an SSH bookmark.
lang.homeFaq3Q: How do I configure AI assistant?
lang.homeFaq3A: Open AI settings, enter your API URL, model name, and API key. Works with any OpenAI-compatible API.
lang.homeAuthorDesc1: electerm is created and maintained by ZHAO Xudong.
